lessonTitle: All That the Lord Hath Spoken We Will Do
quickInsight: God didn't give up on Israel after the golden calf — He won't give up on you.

quotableVerses.text: All that the Lord hath said will we do, and be obedient.
The Lord God, merciful and gracious, longsuffering, and abundant in goodness and truth.
Now therefore, if ye will obey my voice indeed, and keep my covenant, then ye shall be a peculiar treasure unto me above all people.
Listen to him who is the advocate with the Father, who is pleading your cause before him—Saying: Father, behold the sufferings and death of him who did no sin, in whom thou wast well pleased.

sections.title: The Story
items: Israel camps at the base of Mount Sinai. God speaks the Ten Commandments out loud — thunder, lightning, the whole mountain shaking. The people hear God's actual voice.
They promise 'All that the Lord hath said will we do.' Bold words. A real covenant.
Moses goes up the mountain for 40 days to get instructions for the tabernacle — a place where God could literally dwell among them.
While Moses is up there learning how to bring God closer, the people down below get impatient and build a golden calf to worship instead. 40 days. That's all it took to break their promise.
Moses comes down, breaks the stone tables, but then pleads with God for mercy — and God gives Israel another chance.

sections.title: Why This Matters to You
items: You've made promises too — baptism, sacrament every Sunday, maybe a priesthood ordination. And you've probably broken some of them before the week was out. That's not the end of your story. It wasn't the end of Israel's either.

The golden calf wasn't just a statue. It was choosing something easy and visible over something real but invisible. That's every time you pick the feed over the prayer, the group chat over the prompting, the shortcut over the right thing.

God called Israel His 'peculiar treasure' — not weird, but VALUED. The Hebrew word means a king's personal treasure. You're not just another kid in the ward. God is saying 'you're mine and I chose you.'
The most powerful line in this whole block: God didn't cancel the covenant. He made new stone tables. He always offers a reset.

sections.title: Talk Starters
items: You make a goal on Sunday — read scriptures, be nicer to your brother, stop looking at stuff you shouldn't. By Wednesday it's gone. Why do we 'turn aside quickly' like Israel did? What makes commitments fall apart?
President Nelson said the Sabbath is a 'sign between you and God.' If God watched how you spent last Sunday, what sign did you send Him? What sign do you WANT to send?
The Israelites built the calf because Moses was gone 40 days and they got scared and impatient. What's your version of that? When has waiting on God felt too long?
God says 'have no other gods before me.' Nobody here worships a golden calf. But what do you give your best hours, energy, and attention to? Is God actually first or just on the list?

sections.title: How to Teach It
items: Phone timer: Set a 40-second timer. Tell them to sit in silence and wait. It feels forever. Now imagine 40 DAYS with no word from your leader, stuck in a desert, enemies ahead of you. That's why Israel panicked. The lesson: faith means trusting God when you can't see what He's doing.

Two promises: Have each boy write one promise they've made to God and one promise they've broken. Don't share them. Then read Exodus 34:6-7 — God's response isn't anger, it's mercy. Rip up the broken promise paper. That's repentance.
Rank the Ten Commandments: Give them the list from Exodus 20 and ask which one is hardest for teenagers right now. No Sunday School answers. Let them be honest. Then ask why God emphasized THESE ten.

sections.title: The Sabbath Sign
items: President Nelson stopped making Sabbath 'do and don't' lists. Instead he asked one question: 'What sign do I want to give to God?' That one question replaces every rule.
For a 14-year-old, this is freedom. You don't need a checklist. You need one filter: does this show God I'm His?
Challenge them to try the one-question filter this Sunday. Every choice — what to watch, who to text, whether to pay attention in sacrament meeting — run it through 'what sign am I sending?'

sections.title: Restoration Connection
items: The first set of stone tables had the higher law and Melchizedek Priesthood ordinances. When Israel rejected that, God gave a lesser law to prepare them (D&C 84:17-27). You hold the Aaronic Priesthood — the same preparatory priesthood. It's training you for the higher covenants ahead.

The Savior is your Advocate — D&C 45:3-5 shows Him pleading YOUR cause before the Father. Just like Moses stood between God and Israel, Christ stands between God and you. That's what the sacrament points to every week.
Mosiah 15:9 — Christ 'stands betwixt them and justice' the same way Moses stood between Israel and destruction. Every time you take the sacrament, you're accepting that second set of tables.

sections.title: Challenge for the Week
items: Pick ONE of the Ten Commandments you struggle with most. Write it on a sticky note and put it on your mirror. Every morning this week, ask yourself: 'What sign am I giving God today?'

Before sacrament meeting this Sunday, spend 2 minutes in the foyer thinking about what covenant you're renewing. Don't just show up — show up prepared, like the Israelites were told to do before meeting God.
